QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_57

question.text: A contract note without mentioning consideration (trade value) is:

answer.text: Null and void under the Indian Contracts Act
Valid for cash market trades only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A contract note without consideration is null and void under the Indian Contracts Act. Hence, all contracts must mention the consideration separately, along with all statutory charges (STT, GST, stamp duty), regulatory levies, and brokerage. **The Trap:** The contract note must also contain the complete address, phone numbers, email IDs, compliance officer details, and PAN of the broker. Missing any of these details may not void the contract but violates regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers legal requirements of contract notes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Contract note essentials: unique serial number, client name/PAN/UCC, order number/time, trade number/time, quantity, price/rates, consideration, brokerage, STT, GST, stamp duty, SEBI fees, exchange charges. Weighted-average-price contracts need an annexure with individual trade details.
